tools.description: Add a new page to the Figma document.

index.description: Position index to insert the page (0 = first). Defaults to last position.

tools.name: add_page

tools.description: Add a new mode to an existing variable collection (e.g. Light/Dark, Desktop/Mobile). IMPORTANT — Figma free plan only allows 1 mode per collection; calling this tool on a free-plan account will return the error 'Limited to 1 modes only'. If that error occurs, stop retrying and switch to the name-prefix workaround: keep the single default mode and create variables prefixed by mode, e.g. 'light/color-bg' and 'dark/color-bg' in the same collection. Tell the user that native multi-mode variables require a paid Figma plan (Professional or above).
tools.name: add_variable_mode

tools.description: Apply an existing local style (paint, text, effect, or grid) to a node, linking the node to that style.

nodeId.description: Target node ID in colon format e.g. 4029:12345

target.description: For paint styles only — apply to 'fill' (default) or 'stroke'

tools.name: apply_style_to_node

tools.description: Rename multiple nodes using find/replace, regex substitution, or prefix/suffix addition.

find.description: String (or regex pattern when useRegex=true) to search for in the node name

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

prefix.description: String to prepend to the node name

regexFlags.description: Regex flags e.g. 'gi' (default 'g'). Only used when useRegex=true.

replace.description: Replacement string. Required when find is provided.

suffix.description: String to append to the node name

useRegex.description: Treat find as a regular expression (default false)

tools.name: batch_rename_nodes

tools.description: Bind a local variable to a node property so the property is driven by the variable's value. COLOR variables: use fillColor or strokeColor. BOOLEAN variables: use visible. FLOAT variables: use opacity, rotation, width, height, cornerRadius, topLeftRadius, topRightRadius, bottomLeftRadius, bottomRightRadius, strokeWeight, itemSpacing, paddingTop, paddingRight, paddingBottom, paddingLeft.

field.description: Property to bind: fillColor | strokeColor | visible | opacity | rotation | width | height | cornerRadius | topLeftRadius | topRightRadius | bottomLeftRadius | bottomRightRadius | strokeWeight | itemSpacing | paddingTop | paddingRight | paddingBottom | paddingLeft

nodeId.description: Target node ID in colon format e.g. 4029:12345

tools.name: bind_variable_to_node

tools.description: Clone an existing node, optionally repositioning it or placing it in a new parent.

nodeId.description: Source node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

parentId.description: Parent node ID for the clone. Defaults to same parent as source.

tools.name: clone_node

tools.description: Convert an existing FRAME node into a reusable COMPONENT. The frame is replaced in place by the new component.

name.description: Optional name for the component. Defaults to the frame's current name.

nodeId.description: FRAME node ID to convert, in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: create_component

tools.description: Create a new local effect style (drop shadow, inner shadow, or blur).

color.description: Shadow color as hex e.g. #000000 (default #000000, shadows only)

offsetX.description: Shadow X offset in pixels (default 0, shadows only)

offsetY.description: Shadow Y offset in pixels (default 4, shadows only)

radius.description: Blur radius in pixels (default 8 for shadows, 4 for blurs)

spread.description: Shadow spread in pixels (default 0, shadows only)

type.description: Effect type: DROP_SHADOW (default), INNER_SHADOW, LAYER_BLUR, or BACKGROUND_BLUR

tools.name: create_effect_style

tools.description: Create a new ellipse (circle/oval) on the current page or inside a parent node.

parentId.description: Parent node ID in colon format. Defaults to current page.

tools.name: create_ellipse

tools.description: Create a new frame on the current page or inside a parent node.

counterAxisAlignItems.description: Cross-axis alignment: MIN, CENTER, MAX, or BASELINE

counterAxisSpacing.description: Gap between wrapped rows/columns (only when layoutWrap is WRAP)

layoutMode.description: Auto-layout direction: HORIZONTAL, VERTICAL, or NONE

parentId.description: Parent node ID in colon format. Defaults to current page.

primaryAxisAlignItems.description: Main-axis alignment: MIN, CENTER, MAX, or SPACE_BETWEEN

tools.name: create_frame

tools.description: Create a new local layout grid style.

alignment.description: Alignment: STRETCH (default), CENTER, MIN, or MAX (COLUMNS/ROWS only)

color.description: Grid line color as hex e.g. #FF0000 (GRID only, default #FF0000)

count.description: Number of columns or rows (COLUMNS/ROWS only, default 12)

gutterSize.description: Gutter size in pixels (COLUMNS/ROWS only, default 16)

pattern.description: Grid pattern: GRID (default), COLUMNS, or ROWS

sectionSize.description: Grid cell size in pixels (GRID only, default 8)

tools.name: create_grid_style

tools.description: Create a new local paint style with a solid fill color.
tools.name: create_paint_style

tools.description: Create a new rectangle on the current page or inside a parent node.

parentId.description: Parent node ID in colon format. Defaults to current page.

tools.name: create_rectangle

tools.description: Create a Figma Section node on the current page. Sections are the modern way to organize frames and groups on a page.
tools.name: create_section

tools.description: Create a new text node on the current page or inside a parent node. The font is loaded automatically before insertion. Returns the created node ID and bounds. Use set_text to update the content of an existing text node.

fillColor.description: Text color as hex e.g. #000000 (default black)

fontFamily.description: Font family name e.g. 'Inter', 'Roboto', 'SF Pro Display' (default Inter). Must be a font installed in Figma.

fontStyle.description: Font style variant e.g. 'Regular', 'Bold', 'Italic', 'Medium', 'SemiBold' (default Regular). Must match an available style for the chosen fontFamily.

name.description: Node name shown in the layers panel (defaults to the text content)

parentId.description: Parent node ID in colon format. Defaults to current page.

tools.name: create_text

tools.description: Create a new local text style (typography preset). Returns the new style's ID. Apply it to nodes with apply_style_to_node. Use get_styles to list existing text styles.

description.description: Optional human-readable description shown in the Figma style panel

fontFamily.description: Font family name e.g. 'Inter', 'Roboto' (default Inter). Must be installed in Figma.

letterSpacingUnit.description: Letter spacing unit: PIXELS (default) or PERCENT

letterSpacingValue.description: Letter spacing value (unit set by letterSpacingUnit)

lineHeightUnit.description: Line height unit: PIXELS (default) or PERCENT

lineHeightValue.description: Line height value (unit set by lineHeightUnit)

name.description: Style name — use slash notation to organise into groups e.g. 'Heading/H1', 'Body/Regular'

textDecoration.description: Text decoration: NONE (default), UNDERLINE, or STRIKETHROUGH

tools.name: create_text_style

tools.description: Create a new variable (design token) inside an existing collection. Returns the new variable's ID. Use get_variable_defs to find collection IDs, set_variable_value to set values per mode, and bind_variable_to_node to apply the variable to a node property.

collectionId.description: ID of the variable collection to add this variable to (from get_variable_defs)

name.description: Variable name — use slash notation to group e.g. 'Color/Primary', 'Spacing/MD'

type.description: Variable type: COLOR (hex color), FLOAT (numeric dimension/spacing), STRING (text), or BOOLEAN (true/false toggle)

value.description: Initial value for the first mode. COLOR: hex e.g. #FF5733. FLOAT: number e.g. 16. STRING: text. BOOLEAN: true or false.

tools.name: create_variable

tools.description: Create a new local variable collection with an optional initial mode name. NOTE — Figma free plan limits each collection to 1 mode. If you need Light/Dark (or any multi-mode) theming and the user is on the free plan, do NOT try to call add_variable_mode; instead use the name-prefix workaround: create all variables in a single collection and prefix each variable name with its mode, e.g. 'light/color-bg' and 'dark/color-bg'. Inform the user of this limitation.
tools.name: create_variable_collection

tools.description: Delete one or more nodes. This cannot be undone via MCP — use with care.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs to delete in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: delete_nodes

tools.description: Delete a page from the Figma document. Cannot delete the only remaining page.

pageId.description: Page node ID in colon format e.g. '0:2'

pageName.description: Exact page name to delete (alternative to pageId)

tools.name: delete_page

tools.description: Delete a style (paint, text, effect, or grid) by its ID.
tools.name: delete_style

tools.description: Delete a single variable (provide variableId) or an entire collection and all its variables (provide collectionId). Provide exactly one of the two — not both.

collectionId.description: Collection ID to delete (removes all variables in the collection)

tools.name: delete_variable

tools.description: Detach one or more component instances, converting them to plain frames. The link to the main component is broken; all visual properties are preserved.

nodeIds.description: INSTANCE node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: detach_instance

tools.description: Export multiple frames as a single multi-page PDF file. Each frame becomes one page in order. Ideal for pitch decks, proposals, and slide exports.

nodeIds.description: Ordered list of frame node IDs to export as PDF pages, colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

outputPath.description: File path to write the PDF to, must end in .pdf (relative to working directory or absolute)

tools.name: export_frames_to_pdf

tools.description: Export all design tokens (variables and paint styles) as JSON or CSS custom properties. Ideal for bridging Figma variables into your codebase.
tools.name: export_tokens

tools.description: Find and replace text content across all TEXT nodes in a subtree. Searches the entire current page if no nodeId is given.

find.description: Text string (or regex pattern when useRegex=true) to search for

nodeId.description: Root node ID to scope the search. Defaults to the entire current page.

regexFlags.description: Regex flags e.g. 'gi' (default 'g'). Only used when useRegex=true.

replace.description: Replacement string (use empty string to delete matches)

useRegex.description: Treat find as a regular expression (default false)

tools.name: find_replace_text

tools.description: Get dev-mode annotations in the current document or scoped to a specific node. Returns annotation objects with label text, measurement type, and the ID of the annotated node. Omit nodeId to retrieve all annotations on the current page.

nodeId.description: Optional — scope results to annotations on this node and its descendants, colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: get_annotations

tools.description: Get a depth-limited, token-efficient tree of the current selection or page. Use this instead of get_document when exploring large files. Supports detail levels (minimal/compact/full) and dedupe_components for pages heavy with repeated component instances.

dedupe_components.description: When true, INSTANCE nodes are serialized compactly (mainComponentId + componentProperties + overrides array of differing text/nested content) and unique component definitions are collected once in a top-level componentDefs map. Highly token-efficient for screens with many repeated component instances.

depth.description: How many levels deep to traverse (default 2)

detail.description: Property verbosity: minimal (id/name/type/bounds only), compact (+fills/strokes/opacity), full (everything, default)

tools.name: get_design_context

tools.description: Get the full node tree of the current page (not the whole file — only the active page). Returns all nodes recursively and can be very large. Prefer get_design_context for exploration or when token efficiency matters.
tools.name: get_document

tools.description: List all fonts used in the current page, sorted by usage frequency. Useful for understanding typography without scanning all text nodes.
tools.name: get_fonts

tools.description: Get all components defined in the current Figma file.
tools.name: get_local_components

tools.description: Get metadata about the current Figma document: file name, pages, current page
tools.name: get_metadata

tools.description: Get a single node by ID with full detail. Use get_nodes_info to fetch multiple nodes in one round-trip instead of calling this repeatedly. Node ID must be colon format e.g. '4029:12345', never hyphens.

nodeId.description: Node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: get_node

tools.description: Get full details for multiple nodes by ID in one round-trip. Prefer this over calling get_node repeatedly when you need several nodes.

nodeIds.description: List of node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345', '4029:67890']

tools.name: get_nodes_info

tools.description: List all pages in the document with their IDs and names. Lightweight alternative to get_document.
tools.name: get_pages

tools.description: Get the prototype reactions defined on a node. Returns an array of reaction objects — each has a trigger (e.g. ON_CLICK, ON_HOVER, AFTER_TIMEOUT) and an actions array (navigate to node, open URL, go back, etc.). Use set_reactions to add or replace reactions, remove_reactions to delete them.

nodeId.description: Node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: get_reactions

tools.description: Export a screenshot of one or more nodes as base64-encoded image data (held in memory). Use save_screenshots instead when you want to write images directly to disk without base64 in the response.

format.description: Export format: PNG (default), SVG, JPG, or PDF

nodeIds.description: Optional node IDs to export, colon format. If empty, exports current selection.

tools.name: get_screenshot

tools.description: Get the nodes currently selected in Figma. Returns an empty array if nothing is selected. Use get_design_context or get_node to retrieve deeper detail about a specific node by ID.
tools.name: get_selection

tools.description: Get all local styles in the document (paint, text, effect, and grid). Returns each style's ID, name, type, and properties. Use the style ID with apply_style_to_node or update_paint_style. For design tokens (variables), use get_variable_defs instead.
tools.name: get_styles

tools.description: Get all local variable definitions: collections, modes, and values. Variables are Figma's design token system.
tools.name: get_variable_defs

tools.description: Get the current Figma viewport: scroll center, zoom level, and visible bounds.
tools.name: get_viewport

tools.description: Group two or more nodes into a GROUP. All nodes must share the same parent.

name.description: Optional name for the new group

nodeIds.description: Node IDs to group (minimum 2), in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345', '4029:12346']

tools.name: group_nodes

tools.description: Import a base64-encoded image into Figma as a rectangle with an image fill. Use get_screenshot to capture images or provide your own base64 PNG/JPG.

parentId.description: Parent node ID in colon format. Defaults to current page.

scaleMode.description: Image scale mode: FILL (default), FIT, CROP, or TILE

tools.name: import_image

tools.description: Lock one or more nodes to prevent accidental edits in Figma.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: lock_nodes

tools.description: Move one or more nodes to an absolute canvas position. The same x/y is applied to every node independently (not a relative offset from current position).

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: move_nodes

tools.description: Switch the active Figma page. Provide either pageId or pageName.

pageId.description: Page node ID in colon format e.g. '0:1'

pageName.description: Exact page name to navigate to

tools.name: navigate_to_page

tools.description: Remove prototype reactions from a node. Omit indices to remove all reactions. Provide a zero-based indices array to remove specific reactions (use get_reactions first to see current indices).

indices.description: Zero-based indices of reactions to remove. Omit or pass [] to remove all.

nodeId.description: Node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: remove_reactions

tools.description: Rename a single node by ID. Returns the updated node with its new name. Use batch_rename_nodes to rename multiple nodes at once or to apply find/replace patterns across many nodes.

name.description: New name for the node. Figma supports slash-separated path notation e.g. 'Icons/Arrow/Left' to organise nodes in component panels.

nodeId.description: Node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: rename_node

tools.description: Rename an existing page in the Figma document.

pageId.description: Page node ID in colon format e.g. '0:2'

pageName.description: Current page name to find (alternative to pageId)

tools.name: rename_page

tools.description: Change the z-order (layer stack position) of one or more nodes.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

order.description: Order operation: bringToFront, sendToBack, bringForward, or sendBackward

tools.name: reorder_nodes

tools.description: Move one or more nodes to a different parent frame, group, or section.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs to move in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

parentId.description: Target parent node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:99'

tools.name: reparent_nodes

tools.description: Resize one or more nodes. The same width/height is applied to every node in the list independently. Provide width, height, or both.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: resize_nodes

tools.description: Rotate one or more nodes to an absolute angle in degrees.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

rotation.description: Rotation angle in degrees (positive = counter-clockwise in Figma)

tools.name: rotate_nodes

tools.description: Export screenshots for multiple nodes and write them to the local filesystem. Returns file metadata (path, size, dimensions) — no base64 in the response. Use get_screenshot instead when you need the image data in memory.

format.description: Default export format: PNG (default), SVG, JPG, or PDF

items.description: List of {nodeId, outputPath, format?, scale?} objects

format.description: Export format: PNG, SVG, JPG, or PDF

nodeId.description: Node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

outputPath.description: File path to write the image to

scale.description: Default export scale for raster formats (default 2)

tools.name: save_screenshots

tools.description: Find all nodes of specific types in a subtree, regardless of name. Use search_nodes instead when you need to filter by name.

nodeId.description: Root node ID to scan from, colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: scan_nodes_by_types

tools.description: Scan all TEXT nodes in a subtree and return their content. Shorthand for scan_nodes_by_types with ['TEXT'] — use when you only need text copy from a component or frame.

nodeId.description: Root node ID to scan from, colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: scan_text_nodes

tools.description: Search for nodes by name substring and/or type within a subtree. Use this when you know (part of) the node name. Use scan_nodes_by_types when you want all nodes of a type regardless of name.

nodeId.description: Scope search to this subtree (default: current page), colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

types.description: Filter by Figma node type e.g. ['TEXT', 'FRAME', 'COMPONENT']

tools.name: search_nodes

tools.description: Set or update auto-layout (flex) properties on an existing frame.

counterAxisAlignItems.description: Cross-axis alignment: MIN, CENTER, MAX, or BASELINE

counterAxisSpacing.description: Gap between wrapped rows/columns (only when layoutWrap is WRAP)

layoutMode.description: Auto-layout direction: HORIZONTAL, VERTICAL, or NONE

nodeId.description: Frame node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

primaryAxisAlignItems.description: Main-axis alignment: MIN, CENTER, MAX, or SPACE_BETWEEN

tools.name: set_auto_layout

tools.description: Set the blend mode of one or more nodes (e.g. MULTIPLY, SCREEN, OVERLAY).

blendMode.description: Blend mode: NORMAL, MULTIPLY, SCREEN, OVERLAY, DARKEN, LIGHTEN, COLOR_DODGE, COLOR_BURN, HARD_LIGHT, SOFT_LIGHT, DIFFERENCE, EXCLUSION, HUE, SATURATION, COLOR, LUMINOSITY, PASS_THROUGH

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: set_blend_mode

tools.description: Set layout constraints (pinning behaviour) on one or more nodes relative to their parent.

horizontal.description: Horizontal constraint: MIN (left), MAX (right), CENTER, STRETCH, or SCALE

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

vertical.description: Vertical constraint: MIN (top), MAX (bottom), CENTER, STRETCH, or SCALE

tools.name: set_constraints

tools.description: Set corner radius on one or more nodes. Provide a uniform cornerRadius or individual per-corner values.

cornerRadius.description: Uniform corner radius applied to all corners

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: set_corner_radius

tools.description: Apply one or more effects (drop shadow, inner shadow, layer blur, background blur) directly to a node. Replaces all existing effects. Pass an empty array to clear all effects.

effects.description: Array of effect objects. Each has: type (DROP_SHADOW | INNER_SHADOW | LAYER_BLUR | BACKGROUND_BLUR), radius, color (hex, shadows only), opacity (0–1, shadows only), offsetX, offsetY (shadows only), spread (shadows only), visible (default true)

nodeId.description: Target node ID in colon format e.g. 4029:12345

tools.name: set_effects

tools.description: Set the fill color on a single node (takes one nodeId, not an array). Use mode='append' to stack a new fill on top of existing fills instead of replacing them.

color.description: Fill color as hex: #RRGGBB e.g. #FF5733 or #RRGGBBAA e.g. #FF573380 for 50% alpha

mode.description: 'replace' (default) overwrites all existing fills; 'append' stacks this fill on top of existing ones

nodeId.description: Node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

opacity.description: Fill opacity 0–1 (default 1). Combines multiplicatively with any alpha in the color hex.

tools.name: set_fills

tools.description: Set the opacity of one or more nodes (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque).

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: set_opacity

mode.description: "replace" (default) overwrites all existing reactions; "append" adds to them

nodeId.description: Node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

reactions.description: Array of reaction objects. Each has a 'trigger' and an 'actions' array (plural) of Action objects.

tools.name: set_reactions

tools.description: Set the stroke color and weight on a single node (takes one nodeId, not an array). Use mode='append' to stack a new stroke on top of existing strokes instead of replacing them.

mode.description: 'replace' (default) overwrites all strokes; 'append' stacks on top of existing strokes

nodeId.description: Node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: set_strokes

tools.description: Update the text content of an existing TEXT node.

nodeId.description: TEXT node ID in colon format e.g. '4029:12345'

tools.name: set_text

tools.description: Set a variable's value for a specific mode.

value.description: Value to set. COLOR: hex e.g. #FF5733. FLOAT: number e.g. 16. STRING: text. BOOLEAN: true or false.

tools.name: set_variable_value

tools.description: Show or hide one or more nodes by setting their visibility.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

visible.description: true to show the node, false to hide it

tools.name: set_visible

tools.description: Swap the main component of an existing INSTANCE node, replacing it with a different component while keeping position and size.

componentId.description: Target COMPONENT node ID in colon format (from get_local_components)

nodeId.description: INSTANCE node ID in colon format e.g. 4029:12345

tools.name: swap_component

tools.description: Ungroup one or more GROUP nodes, moving their children to the parent and removing the group.

nodeIds.description: GROUP node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: ungroup_nodes

tools.description: Unlock one or more nodes, allowing them to be edited again.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs in colon format e.g. ['4029:12345']

tools.name: unlock_nodes

tools.description: Update an existing paint style's name, color, or description. Only paint styles support in-place updates — to modify text, effect, or grid styles, use delete_style and recreate them.

color.description: New fill color as hex e.g. #FF5733

tools.name: update_paint_style
